info.title: Cortana AI Public API
info.description: The Cortana AI Public API enables programmatic access to contacts, conversations, messages, agents, appointments, conversions, tracking sessions, tags, custom fields, financial data (Stripe, Shopify, Whop), voice call logs/transcripts, and agency-level business creation. Most endpoints are scoped to a business (`/api/v1/businesses/{businessId}/...`); creating a new business uses `POST /api/v1/businesses`, and **agency business templates** (same as *Copy as template* in the app) are listed with `GET /api/v1/businesses/templates` for use as `templateId` on create. All endpoints require Bearer token authentication.

tags.name: Messages
tags.description: Send and list messages within conversations

tags.name: Stripe
tags.description: Stripe financial data — payments, subscriptions, customers, metrics, products

tags.name: Shopify
tags.description: Shopify e-commerce data — orders, customers, products, analytics, abandoned carts

tags.name: Businesses
tags.description: Create businesses and list agency business templates (programmatic onboarding)

post.description:
Creates a new business under the API key owner's agency (same as adding a business in the Control Centre), including defaults or an optional agency template.

**Authentication:** Bearer API key with the **`businesses:write`** scope. This scope is not included in the default *Chrome Extension* preset — use *Agency / onboarding automation* or add **Businesses → Write** when generating the key.

**Who may call:** The key must belong to an **agency owner**, **superuser**, or **manager with Admin** access. Non-superusers need an **active plan**.

**Templates:** To copy configuration from an agency template created in the UI (*Copy as template*), call **`GET /api/v1/businesses/templates`**, pick an `id`, and pass it as **`templateId`** in this request body.

**After success:** Use the returned `id` as `{businessId}` on routes such as `GET /api/v1/businesses/{businessId}/contacts`.

403.description: Insufficient scope, wrong role, inactive plan, or cannot resolve agency

get.description:
Returns agency **business templates** (the same assets you save with *Copy as template* in the Control Centre). Use a template **`id`** as **`templateId`** on `POST /api/v1/businesses`.

**Privacy:** Pass **`omitSource=true`** to omit `sourceBusinessId` and `sourceBusinessName` when you do not want API consumers to see which client business a template was copied from.

**Authentication:** Bearer API key with the **`businesses:write`** scope (same as create business).

**Who may call:** Same rules as create business — **agency owner**, **superuser**, or **manager with Admin**; non-superusers need an **active plan**.

parameters.name: omitSource
parameters.description: When `true` (or `1` / `yes`), each item omits `sourceBusinessId` and `sourceBusinessName` so the response does not reveal the originating client business.

200.description: Templates for the API key owner's agency

403.description: Insufficient scope, wrong role, inactive plan, or cannot resolve agency

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Full-text search across name, email, phone

requestBody.description: Contact data. At least one of email, phone, or firstName is required.

post.summary: Assign a tag to a contact

delete.summary: Remove a tag from a contact

delete.description: Marks the agent as inactive. The agent record is not physically removed.

get.summary: List sources for a conversion config

get.description: Paginated list of Stripe payments with date range, status, recurring, and customer filters.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by payment status (e.g., succeeded, failed, refunded)

get.description: Paginated list of Stripe subscriptions. Filter by status to see active, canceled, past_due, etc.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by subscription status (active, canceled, past_due, trialing)

get.description: Customer roster with LTV, MRR, subscription status, and churn dates. Sort by -totalRevenue to find highest-value customers.

200.description: Paginated list of customers with LTV data

get.description: Returns daily or monthly SaaS metrics: MRR, ARR, churn rates, retention, ARPU, LTV, trial conversions, revenue breakdowns by product, and 40+ more fields. Required: from and to date range.

parameters.name: stripeAccountId
parameters.description: Filter by specific Stripe account (for multi-account setups)

get.description: Product catalog with pricing tiers. Each product includes its price list.

200.description: Paginated list of products with prices

get.description: Order history with UTM attribution data, line items, and financial status. UTM fields enable cross-referencing with attribution data.

parameters.name: financialStatus
parameters.description: Filter by financial status (paid, pending, refunded, etc.)

get.description: Customer roster with RFM segmentation, acquisition source, and spend data. Filter by rfmSegment to find Champions, At Risk, etc.

parameters.name: rfmSegment
parameters.description: Filter by RFM segment (Champions, Loyal, At Risk, New, etc.)

200.description: Paginated list of customers with RFM data

get.description: Product catalog with revenue, order count, repeat driver score, and average customer CLV. Identifies which products drive repeat purchases.

200.description: Paginated list of products with metrics

get.description: Daily store snapshots with revenue, orders, new/repeat customers, cart abandonment rates, and breakdowns by source. Required: from and to date range.
Abandoned checkout data with line items, UTM attribution, and recovery status.

200.description: Paginated list of abandoned carts with items

get.description: Whop marketplace payment events with product info, user details, and attribution status.

parameters.name: eventType
parameters.description: Filter by event type (e.g., payment.succeeded, membership.went_valid)

get.description: Connected Whop marketplace accounts for this business. Sensitive fields (API keys, webhook secrets) are excluded.
Paginated list of voice call logs with duration, status, contact info, and agent ID. Filter by date range and agent.
Returns call details with duration, status, recording URL, and linked contact info.
Returns the full transcript JSON for a voice call. Transcripts can be large. Returns null if no transcript is available.
Customer roster with LTV, membership status, churn dates. Comparable to Stripe customers. Sort by -totalRevenue for highest-value customers.

200.description: Paginated list of Whop customers with LTV and membership data

get.description: Membership lifecycle data — active, canceled, plan details. Comparable to Stripe subscriptions. Filter by status=canceled to see churn.

200.description: Paginated list of Whop memberships with lifecycle data

BearerAuth.description: API key prefixed with `sk-ak-`. Pass as `Authorization: Bearer sk-ak-...`

id.description: Use as businessId on other API routes

sourceBusinessId.description: Originating business UUID when the template was created from *Copy as template*. Omitted when `omitSource=true`.

sourceBusinessName.description: Originating business display name. Omitted when `omitSource=true`.

businessOwnership.description:
Who owns this business — same as the in-app **Who owns this business?** control.

| Value | Meaning |
|-------|--------|
| `us` | Your agency (in the app this row shows your **company name** or **Us** / Cortana) |
| `client` | **Client / someone else** |

timezone.description: IANA timezone identifier. Same complete set and sort order as **Create business** in the app (searchable combobox by region name).

managedPlan.description: Request SaaS/managed plan; requires Stripe Connect on the agency when true

manageBilling.description: When not using managed SaaS flow, sets initial managed billing flag (stored as managedBilling)

templateId.description: Optional agency template to copy (same as *Copy as template* in the app). List IDs with `GET /api/v1/businesses/templates`.

CreateContact.description: At least one of email, phone, or firstName is required.

CreateEntry.description:
Create a conversion entry. Two modes:

**Direct mode:** Pass eventValue, currency, expense directly.

**Mapped mode:** Pass a `data` object with raw key-value pairs. The source's fieldMappings (configured in the dashboard) automatically maps incoming keys to:
- Entry fields (eventValue, currency, expense, occurredAt)
- Contact fields (email, phone, firstName, lastName, etc.)
- Custom fields (any custom field key defined for the business)

Both modes can be combined — direct values are used as fallbacks if mapping doesn't produce a value.

sourceId.description: The conversion source to send the entry to

contactId.description: The contact this conversion belongs to

eventValue.description: Direct conversion value (used when not using field mappings)

occurredAt.description: When the conversion occurred (defaults to now)

data.description: Raw data object with key-value pairs. Keys are your source-side field names (e.g., 'order_total', 'customer_email'). The source's fieldMappings translates these to internal fields automatically.

error.message: Too many requests. Retry after the reset window.
